name: Spaghetti Squash Salad
recipeInstructions:
1. Pierce the skin of the squash several times with a knife to allow steam to escape while cooking.
2. Place the whole squash in a microwave-safe dish and cook on high for 15 minutes, turning once or twice.
3. Remove from oven or steamer and let stand 10 minutes. A steamer placed on top of the stove works well too.
4. Alternatively, cut the squash in half lengthwise, remove seeds, and place cut side down in a pot with 2 inches of water. Cover and boil for 20 minutes.
5. In a small bowl, combine parsley, olive oil, butter or margarine, and minced garlic. Cook on high for 1 minute (or heat in a pan over medium heat on the stove). Set aside to cool.
6. Sauté mushrooms in 3 tablespoons of butter or margarine until lightly browned.
7. Cut the squash lengthwise and remove seeds and membrane. Gently pull the spaghetti-like strands away from the outer skin.
8. Combine the squash with sautéed mushrooms. Pour the butter mixture over the vegetables and sprinkle with cheese, salt, and pepper. Stir gently to combine.
9. Serve hot or at room temperature. Yield: About 4 servings (per serving: 1 Vegetable Exchange + 1 Fat Exchange).
